sourceMovie.title: War
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: War works as a action movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The action and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Run All Night
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If War clicked for you, Run All Night (2015) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after War?
faq.answer: Try Run All Night, The Jackal, The International—each shares DNA with War while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is War?
faq.answer: War (2007) is best described as Action, Crime, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is War worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
